item.title: The Masoretes: Precision as Devotion
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowJewish scribes preserved the Hebrew Bible by fixing not only its words, but the way those words were to be read, sounded, copied, and guarded.What Was at RiskThe Hebrew Bible did not arrive in the Middle Ages as…

item.title: Infrastructure Is the New Ideology
item.description:
Infrastructure Is the New IdeologyIdeologies used to be courteous enough to introduce themselves.They arrived wearing uniforms, waving banners, printing pamphlets, publishing manifestos, and gathering earnest people in drafty halls to shout about…

item.title: The Return of the Salon
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowIn the eighteenth century, thought moved through rooms.Not platforms. Not feeds. Not infinite comment sections populated by anonymous prophets of misreading. Rooms.Salons in Paris. Coffeehouses in London. Drawing…

item.title: The False Gospel of Constant Access
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowThe phone has made absence nearly impossible.We carry work, family, strangers, outrage, news, and half-formed obligations in our pockets. Everyone can reach us. Everyone can ask something of us. Everyone can…

item.title: Algorithmic Feudalism
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowMedieval lords controlled land.Modern platforms control attention.The analogy is imperfect, which is why it is useful. Perfect analogies are usually slogans in costume. Imperfect analogies require thought, and…

item.title: Lillian Gilbreth: The House Under Measurement
item.description:

item.title: The Christian Case for Private Life
item.description:

item.description:

item.title: The Weight of a Word
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowSpeech has become weather: constant, ambient, and rarely consequential. The Christian tradition does not treat what we say this way at all.It treats it as an act.The opening pages of Genesis tell us that creation…

item.title: The Professionalization of Outrage

item.title: The Spiritual Cost of Living Without Ancestors
item.description:

item.title: The Day Hollywood Realized the Camera Was No Longer the Scarce Resource
item.description:

item.title: Can Ghosts Get Pregnant?
item.description:
nowThe internet pretends to be fast. Always moving, always discarding, constantly reinventing itself. A meme burns through Twitter in hours, an app rockets from nowhere to a billion-dollar valuation, and then evaporates before anyone remembers to…
